text:
Market today: Geopolitical tensions in the Middle East are flaring, driving oil prices higher. This creates a risk-off mood, which typically pressures your concentrated tech/chip book and could amplify drawdowns on margin.

Your holdings: SOXL is down a staggering -44.69% and SPAL -46.6%; both are leveraged and decaying, making their "SELL" status critical. QQQ, SMH, and QQQI are also in the red with "SELL" signals. Remember margin amplifies drops.

Watch: Keep a close eye on oil price movements and any further escalation in Middle East conflicts. Also, re-evaluate the stops on your leveraged holdings.

Bottom line: Prioritize aggressive risk management on your losing, leveraged positions given the current market climate.
plays: Immediately re-evaluate stops on SOXL and SPAL; their current default stops are not protecting your capital given significant losses and leveraged nature.
Consider trimming QQQ, SMH, and QQQI to reduce tech concentration, as all are showing "SELL" status and are losing money.
For a small hedge, consider XOM on scanner's ACCUMULATE (entry 147.87-147.72, stop 147.09) given rising oil prices.
